title: Vim Configuration
subtitle: vimrc, runtime, plugins — where settings live.
summary: Vim reads ~/.vimrc (or ~/.config/nvim/init.vim for Neovim) on startup. Plugins go in plugin folders inside 'runtimepath'. Filetype plugins, syntax files, indent files all auto-load by name.

blocks.text: Vim is configured by a startup script and an extensible *runtime path*. Knowing where files live makes debugging much easier.
`'runtimepath'` is a comma-separated list of directories Vim searches for plugins, syntax files, colorschemes, etc. By default it includes `~/.vim` and `$VIMRUNTIME`.

blocks.title: Debugging Mystery Behavior
blocks.text:
When Vim does something unexpected:

  • `:scriptnames` — list every script that's been loaded, in load order. Find out what set the option.
  • `:verbose set X?` — show value of option X *and* the file where it was last assigned. Top tool.
  • `:verbose imap <C-J>` — show what set a particular mapping (great for figuring out which plugin stole your binding).
  • `vim --noplugin` or `vim -u NONE` — start Vim without any vimrc or plugins. If the bug disappears, it's in your config.

**Plugin managers** (vim-plug, packer, lazy.nvim) all work by adding plugin directories to `'runtimepath'`. The mechanism is the same; only the convenience wrapper differs.
After editing your vimrc, `:source $MYVIMRC` reloads it without restarting Vim.

blocks.title: Options partition into rendering, editing, and behavior
blocks.text: Vim's option set is not flat. Rendering options (`'number'`, `'wrap'`, `'cursorline'`) only affect what the window draws. Editing options (`'shiftwidth'`, `'expandtab'`, `'autoindent'`) feed into the operator/insert layer. Behavior options (`'ignorecase'`, `'hlsearch'`, `'incsearch'`) configure subsystems like search. The `:set` command is one interface, but it dispatches across three subsystems with different reload semantics.
